title: Local Authorities Procurement and Asset Disposal
title_en: Local Authorities Procurement and Asset Disposal

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Local Authorities Procurement and Asset Disposal Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation

provisions.chapter: PART II Scope of the Act
provisions.title: Application

provisions.chapter: PART II Scope of the Act
provisions.title: Responsibility for procurement and disposal of assets
provisions.content: 4. Responsibility for procurement and disposal of assets

definitions.definition: the Chief Executive Officer of a local authority
a committee established under section 11
property owned by a local authority whether tangible or intangible
tender and vice versa , inclusive of quotations
a person participating as a bidder in a public procurement or asset disposal activity

invitation to tender, solicitation documents, statement of requirements or any other documents inviting bidders to participate in procurement or disposal proceedings, and includes documents inviting potential bidders to pre-qualify
a contractor who is a natural person or an incorporated company wholly owned and controlled by persons who are citizens of Botswana
the Evaluation Committee or the Adjudication Committee or both
the unit under the Ministry of Local Government designated as the Competent Authority under section 25
a person who enters into a contract with a procuring and disposing entity

the divestiture of assets, including intellectual and proprietary rights and goodwill, and any other rights of a local authority including sale, rental, lease, franchise, auction, or any combination, however classified
a committee established under section 8
external bidder or foreign company bidding for an undertaking
a city council, a town council, a township authority, a district council, a subdistrict council or an administrative authority
a member of a committee, and includes the Chairperson thereof
a screening process to ensure that the invitations to bid are confined to capable firms
acquisition by purchase, rental, lease, hire purchase, licence, tenancy, franchise, or any other contractual means, of any type of works, services or supplies, or any combination thereof

a user department, division, branch or section of a local authority, including any project unit established by and operating under a local authority, which initiates procurement and is the user of the requirements
a natural person or a company incorporated under the<CS:"Hidden - Grey - 8_2"> Companies Act (Cap. 42:01)

definitions.term: Public Procurement and Asset Disposal Board
definitions.definition: the Board established under section 10 of the <CS:"Hidden - Grey - 8_2"> Public Procurement and Asset Disposal Act (Cap. 42:08)
the office designated as a Secretariat of a committee under section 16
all services, other than works and supplies, including banking services, insurance coverage, travel services, cleaning services, consultancy services

the standard specifications developed under the<CS:"Hidden - Grey - 8_2"> Standards Act (Cap. 43:07), and includes—
( a ) a generic or functional description of the qualities required of a procurement item
a document that contains a full and complete description of the requirement that is the subject of the procurement or disposal

goods, raw materials, products, equipment or objects of any kind and description in solid, liquid or gaseous form, or in the form of electricity, or intellectual and proprietary rights, as well as works or services incidental to the provision of such supplies
